nfrs.title: AI Gateway provider failover latency budget — p95 < 5s from primary error to secondary first chunk
nfrs.description: The AI Gateway MUST route to a secondary provider when the primary provider returns a connection error, HTTP 5xx, or fails to emit a first byte within the primary timeout budget (default 8s; configurable per route).
nfrs.target: p95 < 5s from primary upstream error to first byte from secondary provider
nfrs.measurement: Histogram metric `ai_gateway_failover_latency_seconds{primary_provider, secondary_provider, route}` emitted by `services/ai-gateway/src/router.rs` on every provider switch.

nfrs.title: AI Gateway cost-ledger pre-call check overhead — p99 < 10ms
nfrs.description: Before every upstream provider call, the AI Gateway MUST consult the per-tenant cost-ledger to verify the projected token-cost does not exceed the tenant's monthly budget remainder.
nfrs.target: p99 < 10ms; p95 < 5ms for cost-ledger pre-call admission check

nfrs.title: AI Gateway circuit-breaker trip policy — 3 errors in 30s opens; recovery probe at 60s
nfrs.description: The AI Gateway circuit-breaker per (provider, route) tuple MUST transition from CLOSED → OPEN when 3 consecutive upstream errors occur within a 30-second sliding window. Errors counted: connection-failure, HTTP 5xx, HTTP 429 with no `Retry-After`, and provider-emitted timeouts.
nfrs.target: Open within 30s of 3rd error; first recovery probe at exactly 60s post-open

nfrs.title: VN-PII detector recall floor — recall ≥ 99% on quarterly refresh corpus
nfrs.description: The Vietnamese PII detector plugin MUST achieve recall ≥ 99% on the curated VN-PII test corpus (`services/ai-gateway/tests/fixtures/vn_pii_corpus_v*.jsonl`) covering: CMND/CCCD national IDs, MST tax IDs, Vietnamese bank account numbers, Vietnamese phone numbers (mobile + landline), full names with diacritics, and Hanoi/HCMC street addresses.
nfrs.target: Recall ≥ 99% on the VN-PII test corpus; refreshed quarterly
nfrs.measurement: Test harness `services/ai-gateway/tests/vn_pii_recall_test.rs` loads the JSONL corpus, runs each example through the detector, and computes recall per category and overall.

nfrs.title: ZDR attestation freshness — re-verified weekly per provider
nfrs.description: For every upstream AI provider used by the gateway, an active Zero Data Retention (ZDR) attestation MUST be on file under `docs/compliance/zdr-attestations/<provider>/YYYY-MM-DD.{pdf,sha256}`.
nfrs.target: ZDR attestation last-verified-at age ≤ 7 days per active provider
nfrs.measurement: Cron job `deploy/compliance/zdr-attestation-check.sh` runs nightly; for each provider it fetches the current ZDR URL, computes SHA-256, compares to the on-file attestation hash, and updates `zdr_attestation_last_verified_at` if the hash matches.

nfrs.title: AI Gateway per-tenant cache cross-leak — zero cross-tenant reads under 10k random JWTs
nfrs.description: The AI Gateway prompt/completion cache MUST key every entry by `(tenant_id, persona_version, prompt_hash, model_alias)` — never just `prompt_hash`. The `tenant_id` is taken from the verified JWT, not from any caller-supplied parameter.
nfrs.target: Property test: 0 cross-tenant cache hits over 10,000 random JWT pairings
nfrs.measurement: Property test `services/ai-gateway/tests/cache_cross_tenant_leak_test.rs` (T) runs the 10k-pair generator on every PR; assertion: zero cross-tenant hits.

nfrs.title: AI Gateway streaming first-byte latency — p95 < 2s for non-cached completions
nfrs.description: For streaming chat-completion requests on the cache-miss path, the AI Gateway MUST emit the first SSE event to the caller at p95 < 2s and p99 < 4s measured from inbound HTTP request received to first SSE data line written to the response stream.
nfrs.target: p95 time-to-first-byte (TTFB) < 2s for streaming /v1/chat/completions, cache-miss path
nfrs.measurement: Histogram `ai_gateway_streaming_ttfb_seconds{route, cache_hit, model_alias}` emitted by `services/ai-gateway/src/streaming/sse.rs`. Buckets: 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8.

nfrs.title: AI Gateway token-count post-call reconcile accuracy — within 0.5%
nfrs.description: After every upstream provider call, the AI Gateway MUST reconcile the locally-projected token count (used for cost-ledger admit per NFR-AI-002) against the provider-reported usage in the response `usage` field.
nfrs.target: Reconciled token count within 0.5% of provider-reported usage, averaged over 1000 calls
nfrs.measurement: Gauge `ai_gateway_token_count_drift_pct{provider, model_alias}` — rolling 1000-call average delta. Alarm at > 0.5%.

nfrs.title: AI Gateway residency pinning enforcement — provider region matches tenant policy 100%
nfrs.description: Every tenant MUST carry a residency policy field `residency_region` ∈ {`apac`, `eu`, `us`, `global`} on the tenants table. The AI Gateway reads this from the JWT or from a cached lookup.
nfrs.target: 100% of upstream calls route to a provider region matching the tenant residency policy

nfrs.title: AI persona-version drift detection — LangSmith cosine deviation > 0.30 triggers alert
nfrs.description: Every active CUO persona MUST carry a versioned eval set (≥ 50 representative prompts per persona) committed under `modules/cuo/personas/<role>/evals/v<N>.jsonl`.
nfrs.target: Drift alert fires within 24h of cosine deviation > 0.30 on a persona's eval set
nfrs.measurement: LangSmith dataset `cyberos-persona-drift-<role>` updated continuously from the 1% sample.

nfrs.title: AUTH admin endpoint admission latency — verify_jwt + RBAC check < 50ms p95
nfrs.description: The AUTH service MUST complete the admission pipeline `verify_jwt → RBAC.check → admit` at p95 < 50ms and p99 < 100ms measured at the service ingress, over a 14-day rolling window, for every `/v1/admin/*` route.
nfrs.target: p95 < 50ms for verify_jwt + RBAC admit on /v1/admin/* routes
nfrs.measurement: Histogram `auth_admit_latency_seconds{route, result}` emitted by `services/auth/src/middleware/admit.rs` over the verify→check→admit span.

nfrs.title: AUTH RLS cross-tenant leak — zero rows leaked under property test sweep of 1k random tenants
nfrs.description: Every tenant-scoped Postgres table MUST carry a Row-Level Security (RLS) policy `tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id')::uuid` applied to SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE.
nfrs.target: Property test: 0 cross-tenant rows returned across 1000 random tenant_id pairings on every RLS-protected table
nfrs.measurement: Counter `auth_rls_unscoped_query_total` — should always be zero. Sev-0 alarm on any non-zero.

nfrs.title: AUTH JWT signature verification time — < 1ms p99 single key; < 5ms p99 with 7-day rotation window
nfrs.description: AUTH JWT signature verification using the active EdDSA (Ed25519) signing key MUST complete at p99 < 1ms on the production hardware profile (2-vCPU, 4GB RAM container).
nfrs.target: Single-key verify p99 < 1ms; multi-key (during 7-day rotation) p99 < 5ms

nfrs.title: AUTH HIBP API timeout policy — 2s hard timeout; fail-open with audit row on timeout
nfrs.description: The HaveIBeenPwned (HIBP) k-anonymity API call MUST be wrapped in a 2s hard timeout. If HIBP doesn't respond in 2s, the call is aborted.
nfrs.target: HIBP API call wrapped in 2s hard timeout; timeout never blocks signup; audit row emitted on every timeout
nfrs.measurement: Counter `auth_hibp_timeout_total` — incremented on every 2s abort. Sev-3 alarm at > 10/hour.

nfrs.title: AUTH RoleMatrix refresh cadence — ≤ 60s revocation latency; alert on 3 consecutive failures
nfrs.description: The `services/auth/src/rbac/refresher.rs` background task MUST call `RoleMatrix::load_from_db()` every `AUTH_RBAC_REFRESH_SECS` seconds (default 60s, floor 5s).
nfrs.target: RoleMatrix refresher runs every AUTH_RBAC_REFRESH_SECS (default 60s); 3 consecutive failures triggers alert

nfrs.title: AUTH TOTP drift tolerance — accept ±1 step; reject ±2; rate-limit retries
nfrs.description: The TOTP verify endpoint MUST accept codes within ±1 30-second step (effectively a 90-second window: current ± 30s).
nfrs.target: Accept TOTP codes within ±1 30s step (90s window); reject ±2; rate-limit 5 attempts per minute

nfrs.title: AUTH signing-key rotation — active key always present; old key honored 7 days post-rotation
nfrs.description: The AUTH signing-key rotation procedure MUST ensure at least one key with `status=active` is present in JWKS at every moment during rotation — there is never a window where JWKS is empty or has no active key.
nfrs.target: Rotation operation completes with at least one active key on JWKS at all times; old key marked retired but honored for verify for 7 days
nfrs.measurement: Gauge `auth_signing_keys_active_count` — should always be ≥ 1. Sev-0 alarm on 0.

nfrs.title: AUTH Idempotency-Key TTL — 24-hour replay window; hourly cleanup sweeper
nfrs.description: Idempotency-Key handling on admin POST endpoints (per FR-AUTH-001 §1 #6) MUST retain seen keys for exactly 24 hours from first-seen-at timestamp.
nfrs.target: Idempotency keys retained 24h; cleanup sweeper runs hourly removing expired keys
nfrs.measurement: Gauge `auth_idempotency_keys_total` — current row count. Sev-3 alarm at > 1M.

nfrs.title: AUTH bcrypt cost — ≥ 12 rounds; tunable via AUTH_BCRYPT_COST env
nfrs.description: Password hashing in AUTH MUST use bcrypt with cost factor ≥ 12 (default 12; configurable via `AUTH_BCRYPT_COST` env var).
nfrs.target: Password hashing uses bcrypt cost ≥ 12; production override via AUTH_BCRYPT_COST env
nfrs.measurement: Histogram `auth_password_hash_seconds` — at cost 12 expect ~250ms; at cost 13 expect ~500ms. Alerts at < 100ms (cost too low) and > 1000ms (cost too high or CPU contention).

nfrs.title: AUTH OIDC state-token TTL — 10-minute hard expiry; CSRF-resistant; one-shot
nfrs.description: OIDC `state` parameters issued during the Authorization Code flow MUST expire exactly 10 minutes after issuance. The callback handler rejects expired states with HTTP 400 `state_expired`.
nfrs.target: OIDC state tokens expire 10 minutes after issuance; single-use; CSRF-bound to session

nfrs.title: CHAT message persist latency — p95 < 100ms from send to DB ack
nfrs.description: From the moment a CHAT POST request lands on the Mattermost fork, the message MUST be committed to durable Postgres storage and the DB ack returned at p95 < 100ms and p99 < 250ms, measured at the application span.
nfrs.target: p95 < 100ms, p99 < 250ms from CHAT POST /api/v4/posts to DB commit ack

nfrs.title: CHAT WebSocket fanout latency — p95 < 200ms from persist to all subscribed clients
nfrs.description: After a message is committed to Postgres (NFR-CHAT-001), the WebSocket fanout MUST deliver it to every currently-connected subscriber on the channel at p95 < 200ms and p99 < 500ms.
nfrs.target: p95 < 200ms from DB persist ack to message delivered to every connected WebSocket subscriber on the channel
nfrs.measurement: Histogram `chat_ws_fanout_latency_seconds{cross_pod}` per (message, subscriber) pair; rolled up to worst-case-per-message.

nfrs.title: CHAT memory bridge replication lag — p95 < 2s from chat message to memory put
nfrs.description: After a chat message is committed to CHAT Postgres (NFR-CHAT-001), the memory bridge plugin MUST replicate the message to memory at p95 < 2s and p99 < 5s, measured from chat commit to memory bridge put ack.
nfrs.target: p95 < 2s, p99 < 5s from chat message persist to memory bridge put committed

nfrs.title: CHAT Mattermost fork upstream-merge cadence — monthly minor rebase; quarterly major
nfrs.description: The CyberOS Mattermost fork MUST be rebased onto upstream minor releases at least monthly (the first business day after the upstream minor ships).
nfrs.target: Fork rebased onto upstream minor monthly; major version quarterly; security CVEs within 14 days
nfrs.measurement: File inspection — `MATTERMOST_UPSTREAM_VERSION.md` must be current; quarterly audit.

nfrs.title: CHAT VN-search recall floor — hybrid lexical + diacritic-folded recall@10 ≥ 0.90
nfrs.description: The CHAT search endpoint MUST support Vietnamese diacritic-folding: a query "Nguyen" MUST match messages containing "Nguyễn". Conversely, a query with full diacritics MUST match diacritic-less messages.
nfrs.target: Hybrid Vietnamese search (lexical + diacritic-folded + vector) recall@10 ≥ 0.90 on VN test corpus

nfrs.title: CHAT DSAR message export completeness — every message subject authored OR received + chain proof
nfrs.description: A CHAT DSAR (Data Subject Access Request) export for subject `S` MUST include every message in the platform where `S` is either the author OR a member of the channel (recipient) at the time the message was posted.
nfrs.target: 100% of messages where subject is author OR recipient are included; memory chain proof attached

nfrs.title: CRM account-contact-deal schema invariants — required fields + closed enum + relational integrity
nfrs.description: The three core CRM tables (`account`, `contact`, `deal`) MUST enforce required fields + closed enum values declared in `modules/crm/schema/`.
nfrs.target: 100% of account/contact/deal rows satisfy schema + relational invariants

nfrs.title: CRM activity-feed ordering — feed MUST be strictly chronologically consistent
nfrs.description: Activity events (call, email, meeting, note, stage-change) for an account/contact/deal MUST be displayed in the feed in strict `committed_at` order (newest first).
nfrs.target: 100% of activity-feed reads return events in strict committed-at order; no out-of-order

nfrs.title: CRM lead-scoring drift — score model MUST be re-evaluated quarterly against actual outcomes
nfrs.description: The lead-scoring model (`FR-CRM-006`) MUST be re-evaluated quarterly against actual won/lost outcomes; AUC ≥ 0.70 to remain in production.
nfrs.target: Quarterly: score AUC vs actual won/lost outcomes ≥ 0.70; score drift trigger ≤ 5pp

nfrs.title: CRM win-loss approval — deals > threshold MUST be approved by CFO before close
nfrs.description: Deals with `amount_vnd > tenant.large_deal_threshold` MUST require CFO signature before transitioning to `won` or `lost`.
nfrs.target: 100% of large deals (> threshold) carry CFO signature before close

nfrs.title: CRM MST validation freshness — VN business accounts MUST have MST validated within 90 days
nfrs.description: Accounts marked `account_type: vn_business` MUST have their MST (tax ID) validated against the GDT lookup within the last 90 days.
nfrs.target: 100% of VN business accounts carry a GDT MST validation within last 90 days

nfrs.title: CRM bank-config audit — account banking changes MUST emit signed audit row
nfrs.description: Changes to an account's banking info (bank, branch, account number, beneficiary name) MUST emit a signed audit row with `{actor_id, account_id, old_banking_hash, new_banking_hash, changed_at}`.
nfrs.target: 100% of account-bank changes emit a signed audit row + trigger CFO notification

nfrs.title: CRM deal-stage history — every stage change MUST be append-only audited
nfrs.description: Every deal stage transition MUST create an append-only audit row with `{deal_id, from_stage, to_stage, actor_id, changed_at, reason?}`.
nfrs.target: 100% of deal-stage transitions create audit rows; history immutable

nfrs.title: CRM conversion-to-engagement integrity — closed deal MUST link to a PROJ engagement
nfrs.description: When a deal transitions to `won`, a PROJ engagement MUST be created or linked within 24h.
nfrs.target: 100% of won deals create or link to a PROJ engagement within 24h

nfrs.title: harness report generation MUST complete in p95 < 5s over a 10⁶-row audit chain
nfrs.description: The `cyberos-cuo harness report` command MUST produce a complete markdown report from a memory audit chain of up to 10⁶ rows in p95 < 5s and p99 < 15s wall-clock time on a single-core x86_64 machine with 8 GB RAM.
nfrs.target: p95 < 5s; p99 < 15s end-to-end `cyberos-cuo harness report --since 30d` on a chain of up to 10⁶ rows + 250 skills
nfrs.measurement: wall-clock duration of `compute_report()` (excluding markdown formatting + disk write)

nfrs.title: stripe-dedup MUST be deterministic + collision-bounded + check < 10ms p95
nfrs.description: `cuo.core.stripe.compute_stripe(skill_name, signal_id, evidence_rows)` MUST be deterministic — same inputs across processes, sessions, and OS versions produce the same stripe id (verified by repeating the call with identical args and asserting string equality).
nfrs.target: stripe collision rate ≤ 2⁻³² (8-hex SHA-256 truncation birthday bound); open/ glob check p95 < 10ms over ≤ 1000 open proposals
nfrs.measurement: Determinism: `test_stripe_determinism` in `modules/cuo/tests/test_refinement_proposal.py` already verifies — same evidence → same stripe across two calls in the same process.

nfrs.title: proposal classifier MUST be deterministic + read-only + test-gate-blocking
nfrs.description: `cuo.core.proposal_applier.classify_proposal(proposal_path, skill_root)` MUST be deterministic — same proposal body + same target SKILL.md → same `Classification` dataclass across runs / processes / sessions.
nfrs.target: classify_proposal: 100% deterministic across runs; 0% mutation of any file; test-gate failure → 100% queue (never auto-apply)
nfrs.measurement: Determinism (already covered by FR-CUO-202 tests): not a separate benchmark — `test_classify_is_read_only` + `test_bump_levels` together prove classification stability.

nfrs.title: workflow signal eval MUST be O(N) — p95 < 2s at 10⁶ audit rows
nfrs.description: `cuo.core.workflow_evolution.compute_workflow_metrics(rows)` MUST be O(N) in the total audit-row count — single pass, dict insertion, no nested re-iteration of `rows`.
nfrs.target: compute_workflow_metrics + evaluate_workflow_signals: O(N) in row count; p95 < 2s wall-clock at N=10⁶ rows × W=50 workflows
nfrs.measurement: Linearity: NEW benchmark `modules/cuo/tests/bench_workflow_evolution.py` runs `compute_workflow_metrics` against synthetic chains of N ∈ {10³, 10⁴, 10⁵, 10⁶} rows.

nfrs.title: CUO catalog scan latency — full persona+workflow scan completes < 3s
nfrs.description: The CUO supervisor's filesystem catalog scan (`modules/cuo/cuo/catalog.py::scan_catalog()`) MUST complete a full pass over `cuo/<persona>/<workflow>.md` + `skill/public/<skill>/SKILL.md` at p95 < 3s for a catalog of up to 500 personas and 5000 workflows.
nfrs.target: p95 < 3s for full cuo/ + skill/ filesystem catalog scan + validation

nfrs.title: CUO memory audit-chain emission — every chain execution MUST emit ≥ 2 rows
nfrs.description: Every CUO chain execution with `--memory-emit` flag set MUST emit one `kind=cuo.step` row per executed step plus one `kind=cuo.chain.end` summary row carrying `{persona, workflow, step_count, started_at, ended_at, outcome}`.
nfrs.target: 100% of executed chains produce 1 row per step + 1 summary row; reconciliation drift < 0.001%

nfrs.title: CUO handler dispatch correctness — workflow pattern maps to single handler
nfrs.description: Every workflow declaring a `pattern:` in its frontmatter MUST dispatch to exactly one Handler subclass (`LinearHandler | TimeCriticalHandler | PerInstanceHandler | MultiOutputHandler | SequentialApprovalHandler | PersonaPairHandler`) — there is no "default" fallback.
nfrs.target: 100% of workflows dispatch to exactly one handler; 0 fallback-to-default in production

nfrs.title: CUO LLM invoker fallback ladder — MockInvoker → SubprocessInvoker → LLMInvoker
nfrs.description: The CUO supervisor's `select_invoker("auto")` function MUST prefer in priority order: (a) `SubprocessInvoker` if `cyberos-skill` binary is on `$PATH`, else (b) `MockInvoker`.
nfrs.target: 100% of invoker selection follows the documented ladder; explicit selection always honoured

nfrs.title: CUO persona-version stamping — every chain MUST record persona+workflow version
nfrs.description: Every CUO chain audit row MUST carry `{persona_slug, persona_version, workflow_slug, workflow_version}` — both slug and version, both for persona and workflow.
nfrs.target: 100% of audit rows carry persona_slug, persona_version, workflow_slug, workflow_version

nfrs.title: CUO workflow-pattern parsing — YAML safe-load + closed enum + clean error reporting
nfrs.description: Workflow YAML frontmatter MUST parse with `yaml.safe_load()` (no full YAML loader, no arbitrary tag execution).
nfrs.target: 100% of workflow frontmatter parses; failed parses produce {file,line,reason} error

nfrs.title: CUO replay safety — replayed chain MUST produce same audit row count + order
nfrs.description: Replaying a previously-executed chain (via `cyberos-cuo execute --replay <chain_id>`) MUST produce the same `step_count`, same `skill_chain`, same `outcome` as the original.
nfrs.target: 100% of replayed chains match original step count + skill chain + outcome

nfrs.title: CUO route decision latency — natural-language → persona+workflow < 1.5s
nfrs.description: The CUO router (`cyberos-cuo route "<query>"`) MUST return a persona+workflow decision at p95 < 1.5s and p99 < 3s for the default Phase-1 two-stage routing algorithm (persona match → workflow match within persona).
nfrs.target: p95 < 1.5s for cyberos-cuo route <natural-language>

nfrs.title: CUO per-step rollback — failed step MUST trigger declared compensations in reverse order
nfrs.description: When a chain step fails AND the chain declares `rollback: <skill_id>` per step, all completed steps' rollback skills MUST execute in strict reverse order before the chain exits.
nfrs.target: 100% of step failures with declared compensations execute the rollback in reverse order

nfrs.title: CUO LangGraph Postgres checkpointer durability — checkpoint writes MUST be fsync'd
nfrs.description: LangGraph state checkpoints MUST be written to Postgres with `synchronous_commit = on`; checkpoint writes that return success are durable.
nfrs.target: 0 lost checkpoints after process crash; recovery resumes from last fsync'd checkpoint

nfrs.title: DOC eIDAS QTSP attestation — signatures MUST be QES-grade for EU jurisdiction docs
nfrs.description: Signatures applied to documents tagged `jurisdiction: EU` MUST be Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES) per eIDAS Regulation EU 910/2014, backed by a QTSP from the EU trusted list.
nfrs.target: 100% of EU-jurisdiction signatures carry a valid QES attestation from a recognised QTSP

nfrs.title: DOC PAdES-LT timestamp freshness — restamp before any timestamp expiry
nfrs.description: Documents signed with PAdES-LT (Long-Term) MUST be restamped (PAdES-LTA extension) at least 30 days before any contained timestamp or CRL nears its validity end.
nfrs.target: 100% of PAdES-LT documents restamped at least 30 days before any contained timestamp expires

nfrs.title: DOC VN-CA signature compliance — Vietnamese signatures MUST chain to NEAC root
nfrs.description: Documents tagged `jurisdiction: VN` MUST be signed with certificates that chain to a National Electronic Authentication Centre (NEAC) recognised root CA.
nfrs.target: 100% of VN-jurisdiction signatures chain to a NEAC-recognised CA

nfrs.title: DOC VNeID linkage — identity-verified signers MUST be bound to VNeID identifier
nfrs.description: When a signer's identity is verified via VNeID (Vietnamese national digital ID), the resulting signature MUST carry the VNeID identifier in the attestation block.
nfrs.target: 100% of VNeID-verified signers carry the VNeID identifier in the signature attestation

nfrs.title: DOC IDV method coverage — all 4 declared methods MUST be reachable per tenant
nfrs.description: The four declared IDV methods (`vneid`, `eidas`, `aatl`, `email_link`) MUST all be reachable + functional for every tenant in supported regions; outages are tracked per method per region.
nfrs.target: 100% of IDV methods (VNeID, eIDAS, AATL, email-link) reachable from each tenant region
nfrs.measurement: Per-method availability gauges (hourly probe + monthly aggregate).

nfrs.title: DOC expiry alert latency — alerts MUST fire at 90/30/7/1 day(s) before document expiry
nfrs.description: Documents with `expiry_at:` metadata MUST trigger alerts at 90, 30, 7, and 1 day(s) before expiry, each within ±1 day of the scheduled point.
nfrs.target: 100% of tracked-expiry documents fire alerts at the four scheduled cadences within ±1 day

nfrs.title: DOC renewal-draft accuracy — generated renewal MUST preserve original terms unless flagged
nfrs.description: The renewal-proposal CUO workflow (`FR-DOC-009`) MUST generate a renewal draft that preserves the original document's core terms unless the workflow explicitly flags a delta.
nfrs.target: 100% of renewal drafts preserve original terms (parties, scope, term length) modulo explicit deltas
nfrs.measurement: Sample audit: CLO-Legal reviews 10% of generated drafts for unexpected mutations; counter `doc_renewal_unexpected_mutation_total`.

nfrs.title: DOC signing workflow integrity — multi-party sign MUST complete in declared order
nfrs.description: Multi-party signing flows declared as `sequence: ordered` MUST require signers to sign in declared order; signer N cannot sign before signers 1..N-1 have completed.
nfrs.target: 100% of sequential-signing workflows complete in declared signer order; 0 out-of-order signatures

nfrs.title: DOC third-party import validation — imported signed docs MUST be signature-verified
nfrs.description: Documents imported from third-party providers (DocuSign, Adobe Sign, etc.) MUST have their signatures verified at import time against the issuing CA chains.
nfrs.target: 100% of imported signed docs verified at import; invalid signatures flagged + quarantined

nfrs.title: EMAIL inbound delivery latency — SMTP receipt to inbox visible < 30s p95
nfrs.description: Inbound emails reaching the Stalwart MTA MUST be parsed, classified, indexed, and visible in the recipient's inbox UI within 30s p95 and 90s p99.
nfrs.target: p95 < 30s from SMTP MAIL FROM to inbox-row visible in user UI

nfrs.title: EMAIL CaMeL prompt-injection blocking — recall ≥ 95% on the held-out adversarial set
nfrs.description: The CaMeL dual-LLM defence (`FR-EMAIL-005`) MUST detect prompt-injection content in inbound mail at recall ≥ 95% on the platform's held-out adversarial corpus (200+ samples curated by CISO).
nfrs.target: Recall ≥ 95% on the platform's adversarial prompt-injection email corpus
nfrs.measurement: Per-quarter benchmark: recall + FP rate against held-out + legitimate corpus.

nfrs.title: EMAIL BIMI freshness — VMC + BIMI record MUST stay valid; auto-alert on expiry
nfrs.description: The tenant's BIMI Verified Mark Certificate (VMC) + DNS BIMI record MUST be validated weekly via the platform's BIMI prober.
nfrs.target: BIMI VMC + DNS record validated weekly; alert ≥ 30 days before expiry

nfrs.title: EMAIL DKIM/SPF/DMARC pass-through — outbound mail MUST be DMARC-aligned
nfrs.description: Every outbound email MUST carry a valid DKIM signature aligned to the tenant's sending domain; the platform refuses to send unsigned mail.
nfrs.target: 100% of outbound emails pass DKIM + SPF + DMARC alignment for the tenant's sending domain
nfrs.measurement: Hourly synthetic mail-to-self DMARC pass rate per tenant.

nfrs.title: EMAIL thread merge correctness — In-Reply-To + References MUST group messages correctly
nfrs.description: Inbound messages carrying `In-Reply-To` or `References` headers MUST be merged into the parent thread when the parent exists in the tenant's inbox.
nfrs.target: ≥ 99% of incoming messages with In-Reply-To are correctly merged into the parent thread

nfrs.title: EMAIL dual-LLM separation — quarantined LLM MUST NOT see untrusted content + tool surface
nfrs.description: The CaMeL dual-LLM architecture MUST maintain strict separation: the "privileged" LLM has tool access but never sees raw email body; the "quarantined" LLM sees raw body but has zero tool access.
nfrs.target: 100% of CaMeL pipeline runs maintain LLM separation; 0 untrusted-content-with-tools events

nfrs.title: EMAIL DSAR export completeness — export MUST contain all subject's messages + metadata
nfrs.description: A DSAR export for a data subject MUST include all email messages where the subject appears in `From`, `To`, `Cc`, `Bcc`, or as referenced in body text via PII matching.
nfrs.target: 100% of DSAR exports include all related messages; reconciliation = 0 missing rows

nfrs.title: EMAIL bulk-send approval — sends > 100 recipients MUST require explicit approval
nfrs.description: Outbound sends with > 100 unique recipients MUST be paused for explicit approval by a tenant role granted `email:bulk:approve`.
nfrs.target: 100% of bulk sends (>100 recipients) carry a recorded approval; 0 auto-sent bulks

nfrs.title: HR member schema invariants — required fields + closed enum types + unique member_id
nfrs.description: The HR `member` table MUST enforce: required fields (`member_id, full_name, email, employment_status, hire_date, contract_type`); closed enum for `employment_status` and `contract_type`.
nfrs.target: 100% of member rows satisfy required-field + uniqueness invariants

nfrs.title: HR contract-history immutability — contract changes MUST create new versions, never edit old
nfrs.description: Contract changes (promotion, comp change, role change) MUST create a new contract row with `effective_from` timestamp; the prior row is closed with `effective_to`.
nfrs.target: 100% of contract changes create new versioned row; 0 in-place edits

nfrs.title: HR CCCD-photo access logging — every read MUST be logged with reason; sev-1 on bypass
nfrs.description: Every read of a CCCD (Vietnamese national ID) photo MUST be logged with `{accessor_id, accessed_at, member_id, reason_code, justification}`.
nfrs.target: 100% of CCCD-photo reads carry a logged access reason; 0 bypassed reads

nfrs.title: HR leave-balance accuracy — accrued - used = balance within ±0.01 days
nfrs.description: Per-member per-leave-type balance MUST satisfy: `accrued - used - corrections = current_balance`, within ±0.01 days.
nfrs.target: 100% of member leave balances match accrual - usage within ±0.01 days

nfrs.title: HR policy-version compatibility — policy changes MUST be backward-compatible for in-flight cases
nfrs.description: HR policies (leave, working hours, OT rates) MUST be versioned; in-flight cases (a leave request submitted under v1) MUST be evaluated against v1 even after v2 ships.
nfrs.target: 100% of in-flight HR cases preserve their original policy version
nfrs.measurement: Counter `hr_policy_version_mismatch_total` — case evaluated under wrong version; must be 0.

nfrs.title: HR accrual-correction audit — every manual leave adjustment MUST require reason + approver
nfrs.description: Manual leave-accrual corrections (overrides of the automatic accrual) MUST be approved by a member with `hr:leave:adjust` permission.
nfrs.target: 100% of accrual corrections carry approver signature + structured reason

nfrs.title: HR onboarding saga durability — every step MUST be replayable; crashes recoverable
nfrs.description: The HR onboarding saga (`FR-HR-007`) MUST persist step state in a durable journal — process crashes resume at last completed step.
nfrs.target: 100% of onboarding sagas reach terminal state; crashes recover from last step

nfrs.title: HR termination signoff window — termination MUST be signed by CHRO + line manager within 72h
nfrs.description: Termination workflow MUST require both CHRO + line-manager signatures within a 72h rolling window.
nfrs.target: 100% of terminations carry both signatures within 72h; expired approvals require resubmission

nfrs.title: HR performance history retention — performance signals MUST be retained ≥ 7 years
nfrs.description: Performance signals (reviews, ratings, 360s, PIPs) MUST be retained ≥ 7 years post-member-termination.
nfrs.target: 100% of perf-signal rows retained ≥ 7 years; 0 unauthorized deletions
nfrs.measurement: Gauge `hr_perf_record_oldest_age_years` — must be ≥ 7 for terminated members.

nfrs.title: KB document versioning history — every save MUST create an immutable version row
nfrs.description: Every document save MUST create an immutable version row in `kb_document_version` carrying `{doc_id, version_no, author_id, saved_at, body_hash, body_blob_ref}`.
nfrs.target: 100% of document saves produce a version row; history immutable; retention ≥ 1 year

nfrs.title: KB share-link revocation — revoked link MUST stop working within 60s globally
nfrs.description: Revoking a KB share link MUST propagate to all read paths (origin + CDN + cached link tables) within 60s p95.
nfrs.target: p95 < 60s from revoke action to refused-access at any edge

nfrs.title: KB semantic search recall — bge-m3 + rerank MUST hit recall@10 ≥ 90%
nfrs.description: KB semantic search (`FR-KB-005` bge-m3 + `FR-KB-006` rerank) MUST achieve recall@10 ≥ 90% on the platform's labeled evaluation corpus (1000+ query/doc pairs).
nfrs.target: Recall@10 ≥ 90% on the platform's labeled query/document corpus
nfrs.measurement: Per-quarter benchmark: recall@10, MRR, latency p95.

nfrs.title: KB lexical search recall — FTS5 + pgroonga MUST cover known-keyword queries at ≥ 99%
nfrs.description: Lexical search (FTS5 for SQLite tier; pgroonga for Postgres tier) MUST find documents containing exact keyword matches at recall ≥ 99% — known-keyword queries should not silently miss documents.
nfrs.target: Recall ≥ 99% for known-keyword queries; p95 latency < 200ms
nfrs.measurement: Per-quarter benchmark: recall on known-keyword test set.

nfrs.title: KB runbook-tag accuracy — runbook docs MUST be findable by incident-keyword + tag combo
nfrs.description: Documents tagged `kind:runbook` MUST be retrievable via the API `?tag=runbook&query=<incident-keyword>` and rank in the top-3 results for the keyword.
nfrs.target: 100% of tagged runbooks surface in top-3 for canonical incident-keyword queries
nfrs.measurement: Per-quarter benchmark: top-3 accuracy on canonical incident queries.

nfrs.title: KB translation parity — translated doc MUST reference + lag source by ≤ 24h
nfrs.description: Documents with `translation_of: <doc_id>` frontmatter MUST point to a real source document in the same tenant.
nfrs.target: 100% of `translation_of:` docs reference an existing source; sync lag ≤ 24h after source change

nfrs.title: KB render cache TTL — rendered HTML MUST refresh within 60s of doc save
nfrs.description: The KB server-side renderer (`FR-KB-002`) MUST cache rendered HTML keyed by `(doc_id, doc_version, theme)` for fast serving.
nfrs.target: p95 < 60s from doc save to fresh rendered HTML served to all readers

nfrs.title: MCP protocol compliance — runtime MUST conform to spec 2025-11-25
nfrs.description: The CyberOS MCP runtime MUST implement the JSON-RPC 2.0 message envelope, capability negotiation, and tool/resource/prompt primitives per the MCP spec version 2025-11-25.
nfrs.target: 100% pass on official MCP conformance suite for spec version 2025-11-25
nfrs.measurement: CI: full conformance suite pass/fail per spec version.

nfrs.title: MCP tool annotation gating — destructive ops require explicit human approval annotation
nfrs.description: Every MCP tool exposed by CyberOS MUST declare annotations: `{destructive: bool, readOnly: bool, openWorld: bool}`.
nfrs.target: 100% of tools annotated destructive=true gate behind human approval; 0 silent destructions

nfrs.title: MCP audience-bound tokens — OAuth tokens MUST be aud-scoped to the MCP server
nfrs.description: Tokens issued for use against an MCP server MUST carry the JWT `aud` claim equal to `mcp:<server-id>` (e.g., `mcp:cyberos-projects`).
nfrs.target: 100% of MCP-bound tokens carry aud=mcp:<server-id>; cross-audience use rejected

nfrs.title: MCP task primitive lifecycle — task state transitions MUST be linear + auditable
nfrs.description: Tasks (per the spec `tasks` primitive) MUST transition through states `pending → running → (succeeded | failed | cancelled)` — no backward transitions; no state outside the closed set.
nfrs.target: 100% of task state transitions follow the spec FSM; 0 backward transitions

nfrs.title: MCP elicitation prompt safety — server-issued prompts MUST be scoped + escapable
nfrs.description: MCP elicitation prompts (server → client requests for additional info) MUST NOT contain instructions that override the host shell's own system prompt or behaviour rules.
nfrs.target: 100% of elicitation prompts pass injection-safety lint; clients can always decline

nfrs.title: MCP SEP-986 naming compliance — all server/tool identifiers MUST match SEP-986 regex
nfrs.description: Every MCP server, tool, resource, and prompt name MUST conform to the SEP-986 naming pattern: `^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,63}$` (lowercase ASCII, digits, underscore, hyphen, leading letter, max 64 chars).
nfrs.target: 100% of names pass SEP-986 validator; non-conforming names blocked at registration

nfrs.title: MCP OAuth PKCE — authorization code flow MUST require PKCE; no implicit grant
nfrs.description: The MCP-AUTH endpoint MUST require PKCE (RFC 7636) for every authorization code request — `code_challenge` + `code_challenge_method=S256` are mandatory.
nfrs.target: 100% of authorization-code requests carry valid PKCE challenge; implicit grant disabled

nfrs.title: MCP server heartbeat availability — registered servers MUST respond within 2s
nfrs.description: Every registered MCP server MUST respond to the gateway's heartbeat probe (`ping` or equivalent) within 2s p95.
nfrs.target: p95 < 2s for server heartbeat; 99.5% monthly availability per server

nfrs.title: memory Layer-2 ingest lag — p95 < 1s from Layer-1 commit to l2_memory row visible
nfrs.description: From the moment a row is committed to a Layer-1 memory store (per-actor SQLite append-log), the row MUST be visible in the global `l2_memory` Postgres table at p95 < 1s and p99 < 3s.
nfrs.target: p95 < 1s; p99 < 3s end-to-end Layer-1 commit to Layer-2 visibility
nfrs.measurement: Histogram `memory_layer2_ingest_lag_seconds{tenant_id, actor_id}` measured per ingested row as `(l2_committed_at - l1_committed_at)`.

nfrs.title: memory chain-anchor verification — every l2_memory read re-checks anchor; mismatch → quarantine
nfrs.description: Every read from `l2_memory` MUST re-verify the row's chain anchor (hash of `(prev_chain_hash, payload_canonical_json)`) against the on-row `chain_hash` column.
nfrs.target: 100% of l2_memory reads re-verify the chain anchor; any mismatch quarantines the row and sev-1 alerts

nfrs.title: memory per-tenant cursor isolation — cursor advance for tenant A never affects tenant B
nfrs.description: The Layer-2 ingest cursor (`services/memory/src/layer2/cursor.rs`) MUST be scoped per (tenant_id, actor_id) — there is no global cursor that crosses tenants.
nfrs.target: Property test: 0 cross-tenant cursor influence under 1000 random advance sequences

nfrs.title: memory doctor invariant runtime — full invariant pass < 30s on 100k-row store
nfrs.description: The `cyberos-memory doctor` command MUST complete a full invariant scan in < 30s on a Layer-2 store of 100,000 rows. The scan includes: chain-anchor verification (NFR-MEMORY-002), cursor consistency (NFR-MEMORY-003), per-tenant RLS sanity, and PII pre-ingest detection (NFR-MEMORY-007).
nfrs.target: cyberos-memory doctor full invariant pass completes < 30s on a 100k-row test store

nfrs.title: memory deterministic export bit-identity — two runs on same store produce byte-identical zip
nfrs.description: The `cyberos-memory export <tenant_id>` command MUST produce a deterministic ZIP bundle: two runs against the same Layer-2 state MUST produce byte-identical output (verified by SHA-256 of the zip file).
nfrs.target: Two consecutive `cyberos-memory export` runs on an unchanged store produce identical SHA-256 hashes on the output bundle
nfrs.measurement: Test artifact archive — `tests/memory/export_determinism/expected_hashes.txt` lists expected SHA-256s for a battery of test stores.

nfrs.title: memory STH publication frequency — consolidate every 24h or 100MB whichever first
nfrs.description: The memory consolidation process MUST publish a Signed Tree Head (STH) — a Merkle root over the consolidated window's rows, signed by the platform key — every 24 hours of wall-clock time OR every 100MB of new rows ingested since the last STH, whichever threshold is hit first.
nfrs.target: Signed Tree Head (STH) published every 24h OR 100MB of new rows, whichever occurs first

nfrs.title: memory PII pre-ingest gate — Presidio + VN-PII recall ≥ 99% before row hits Layer 2
nfrs.description: Every row entering `l2_memory` MUST first pass through the PII detection pipeline: Presidio (English) followed by the VN-PII plugin (Vietnamese, NFR-AI-004 recall floor).
nfrs.target: 100% of l2_memory rows have been through PII detection at recall ≥ 99% before commit

nfrs.title: memory search API recall floor — lexical hybrid recall@10 ≥ 0.85 on test corpus
nfrs.description: The `services/memory/src/search.rs` search API MUST achieve recall@10 ≥ 0.85 on the curated memory search test corpus (`services/memory/tests/fixtures/search_corpus_v*.jsonl`).
nfrs.target: Hybrid lexical + vector search recall@10 ≥ 0.85 on the memory search test corpus

nfrs.title: RED metric coverage — every service emits rate/error/duration via cyberos-obs-sdk
nfrs.description: Every CyberOS backend service MUST emit the three RED metric families on every public route: `request_rate_total{route, method, status}`, `request_errors_total{route, method, error_class}`, `request_duration_seconds{route, method, status}` (histogram).
nfrs.target: 100% of services emit RED metrics; CI fails if a service lacks the cyberos-obs-sdk middleware
nfrs.measurement: Prometheus query `count by (service) (request_rate_total)` lists services emitting RED. Expected: equal to count of deployed services from the manifest.

nfrs.title: Trace continuity — W3C traceparent propagates across ≥ 2 service hops; CI test enforces
nfrs.description: Every CyberOS backend service MUST propagate the W3C `traceparent` and `tracestate` HTTP headers on outbound calls (server → service → service) and on NATS subjects (via the `traceparent` message header).
nfrs.target: 100% of cross-service requests carry an unbroken traceparent chain ≥ 2 hops
nfrs.measurement: Counter `obs_trace_propagation_gap_total{from_service, to_service}` emitted when an inbound request lacks `traceparent` but came from a known-internal source.

nfrs.title: Tail-sampling efficiency — 100% errors + 10% normal; sampler CPU overhead < 2%
nfrs.description: The OTel collector tail-sampler MUST apply the FR-OBS-006 policies (100% errors, 100% 5xx, 100% slow, 100% flagged-tenants, 10% normal) and the measured sampling rates MUST match the policy targets within ±1% over a 1-hour window.
nfrs.target: Tail sampler CPU overhead < 2% of collector CPU; sampling rates exact per FR-OBS-006 policy
nfrs.measurement: Collector self-metric `otelcol_processor_tail_sampling_count_traces_sampled{policy}` divided by `otelcol_processor_tail_sampling_count_traces_seen` per minute. Should match policy targets ±1%.

nfrs.title: Alert-to-CUO-runbook routing latency — p95 < 30s from rule fire to runbook surface
nfrs.description: From the moment a Prometheus alert rule transitions to `firing`, the CyberOS routing pipeline MUST surface the matched CUO runbook to the on-call surface (Slack channel + CUO supervisor) at p95 < 30s and p99 < 60s.
nfrs.target: p95 < 30s from Alertmanager rule fire to CUO runbook surfaced to on-call
nfrs.measurement: Histogram `obs_alert_to_runbook_seconds` measured from Alertmanager's `startsAt` to the Slack-webhook delivery timestamp on the routed message.

nfrs.title: LangSmith AI-trace retention — 90-day window, queryable by persona_version
nfrs.description: Every AI Gateway call (1% sample per NFR-AI-010 + 100% of error/slow calls per NFR-OBS-003) MUST be exported to LangSmith with tags `{tenant_id, persona_version, route, model_alias, status}`.
nfrs.target: AI traces queryable for 90 days; filter by persona_version returns results in < 5s
nfrs.measurement: LangSmith project-config audit (`docs/compliance/langsmith-config.md`) lists current retention; reviewed quarterly.

nfrs.title: OBS compliance view scoping — per-tenant RLS-equivalent on Grafana proxy
nfrs.description: The tenant-aware Grafana proxy MUST rewrite every PromQL, LogQL, and TraceQL query to inject a `tenant_id=<tenant_from_jwt>` filter before forwarding to Prometheus, Loki, or Tempo respectively (NFR-OBS-008 covers the AST coverage).
nfrs.target: Tenant A user views ONLY tenant A data in Grafana; zero cross-tenant leakage under property test
nfrs.measurement: Counter `obs_proxy_unscoped_query_total{role}` — should be zero for any role lacking `tenant_scope=global`.

nfrs.title: Chain-of-custody manifest completeness — every alert carries trace_id + alertmanager_id + runbook_id
nfrs.description: Every Alertmanager-routed alert MUST carry the four-field chain-of-custody manifest:
nfrs.target: 100% of fired alerts carry the four-field custody manifest; CI verifies on every release
nfrs.measurement: Counter `obs_alert_custody_manifest_complete_total{result}` where result ∈ {`complete`, `incomplete`}. Incomplete should be zero.

nfrs.title: Grafana proxy AST-injection coverage — PromQL + LogQL + TraceQL all rewritten with tenant_id
nfrs.description: The tenant-aware Grafana proxy MUST parse incoming queries into the language-specific AST (PromQL, LogQL, TraceQL) — MUST NOT use regex-based rewriting which is bypassable via comment-injection.
nfrs.target: 100% of PromQL, LogQL, TraceQL queries through the proxy carry an injected tenant_id filter (verified per-query-language test suite)
nfrs.measurement: Counter `obs_proxy_ast_parse_failed_total{language}` — should be near-zero; high values indicate AST parser drift.

nfrs.title: OKR objective-KR schema invariants — KR types closed enum; numeric KRs measurable
nfrs.description: KR types MUST be a closed enum: `numeric`, `binary`, `milestone-progress`, `custom-sql`. Ad-hoc types are rejected at create time.
nfrs.target: 100% of KRs use the closed enum types; 100% of numeric KRs are computable from declared source

nfrs.title: OKR custom-SQL co-sign — custom-SQL KRs MUST require CTO + objective-owner co-sign
nfrs.description: KRs using `custom-sql` progress source MUST be co-signed by CTO + the objective owner before activation; the SQL is reviewed for safety + correctness.
nfrs.target: 100% of custom-SQL KRs carry both signatures + readonly DB role

nfrs.title: OKR auto-progress accuracy — batch-computed progress MUST match per-KR sample within ±2%
nfrs.description: The nightly auto-progress batch (`FR-OKR-004`) MUST produce per-KR progress values within ±2% of an independent re-computation.
nfrs.target: 100% of auto-progress computations within ±2% of independent per-KR computation
nfrs.measurement: Daily reconciliation: batch values vs sample-recomputed; counter `okr_progress_delta_pct{kr}` histogrammed.

nfrs.title: OKR KR check-in latency — manual check-in MUST persist within 2s
nfrs.description: Manual KR check-ins (`FR-OKR-005`) MUST persist + appear in the OKR dashboard within 2s p95.
nfrs.target: p95 < 2s from check-in submit to visible in the OKR dashboard

nfrs.title: OKR digest-recipient freshness — Monday digest list MUST match current tenant roster
nfrs.description: The Monday OKR digest (`FR-OKR-006`) MUST be delivered only to currently-active members; terminated members MUST be removed within 24h of HR transition.
nfrs.target: 100% of digest recipient lists reflect terminations + role changes within 24h

nfrs.title: OKR cycle-phase advance correctness — cycle phases MUST advance per declared schedule
nfrs.description: OKR cycles MUST advance through declared phases: `draft → active → mid-cycle-check → final-check → retro-draft → closed` on the declared schedule.
nfrs.target: 100% of cycles advance phase per schedule; 0 stuck or auto-jumped phases

nfrs.title: OKR retro-approval gate — quarterly retro MUST be CEO-signed before closing
nfrs.description: A cycle MUST NOT advance to `closed` without a CEO-signed retro.
nfrs.target: 100% of closed cycles carry a CEO-signed retro before close

nfrs.title: OKR objective cascade integrity — child objectives MUST chain to parent at all times
nfrs.description: Objectives declaring `parent: <objective_id>` MUST reference a real, active objective in the same tenant.
nfrs.target: 100% of child objectives have a resolvable parent; 0 orphan trees

nfrs.title: PROJ Yjs CRDT convergence — concurrent edits MUST converge to identical state across all clients
nfrs.description: Concurrent edits to the same project artifact (issue, brief, cycle plan) via Yjs CRDT MUST converge to byte-identical state across all connected clients within 2s after the last network exchange.
nfrs.target: 100% of concurrent edit sets converge within 2s of last network exchange
nfrs.measurement: Histogram `proj_yjs_convergence_latency_seconds` — measured by synthetic multi-client probes.

nfrs.title: PROJ billable cascade correctness — rate-card change MUST propagate to all unbilled time
nfrs.description: When a rate-card row is created/updated/deactivated, all unbilled time entries falling under that rate-card's scope MUST recompute their `billable_amount` within 60s.
nfrs.target: 100% of unbilled time entries reflect the current rate-card within 60s of rate change
nfrs.measurement: Histogram `proj_billable_cascade_latency_seconds` — time from rate change to last entry recomputed.

nfrs.title: PROJ memory_link integrity — every memory_link MUST resolve to a real memory row
nfrs.description: Every `memory_link` field on a PROJ entity (issue, decision-anchor, cycle review) MUST reference a real, accessible memory row by its `(tenant_id, actor_id, seq)` triple.
nfrs.target: 100% of memory_link references resolve; 0 dangling links in production

nfrs.title: PROJ cycle-window enforcement — work added mid-cycle MUST be flagged + tracked
nfrs.description: Cycle windows (default 2-week sprint, configurable) MUST be persisted with `{start_at, end_at, frozen_at}` where `frozen_at` is the moment scope is closed.
nfrs.target: 100% of mid-cycle additions/removals tracked + visible in the cycle review draft

nfrs.title: PROJ estimate-calibration freshness — calibration data MUST refresh weekly
nfrs.description: Per-team estimate calibration (`FR-PROJ-013`) MUST refresh weekly using closed issues from the prior cycle window.
nfrs.target: Calibration freshness ≤ 7 days; 100% of teams refreshed within their cadence

nfrs.title: PROJ Gantt critical path correctness — CPM algorithm MUST match reference output
nfrs.description: The Gantt view's critical-path-method (CPM) computation MUST match a reference implementation for any dependency graph the UI accepts (acyclic, single-source, single-sink, or general DAG).
nfrs.target: 100% of Gantt critical-path computations match a reference CPM implementation

nfrs.title: PROJ a11y-ci gate — every page MUST pass axe-core with 0 critical/serious issues
nfrs.description: Every PROJ page MUST pass `axe-core` with 0 critical and 0 serious violations in the CI a11y-ci gate.
nfrs.target: 0 critical/serious axe-core violations per page; 100% page coverage in CI
nfrs.measurement: Per-page counter `proj_axe_violation_count{page, severity}` — critical/serious must be 0.

nfrs.title: PROJ design-tokens drift — UI MUST consume tokens; raw hex/px values are CI-banned
nfrs.description: PROJ UI component source (`apps/proj/src/`) MUST NOT contain raw hex color values (`#...`), raw RGB/HSL strings, or raw pixel values for spacing/typography.
nfrs.target: 0 raw hex or raw px values in PROJ component CSS/JSX; tokens consumed exclusively

nfrs.title: PROJ issue state machine — transitions MUST conform to the declared FSM
nfrs.description: Issue state transitions MUST conform to the FSM declared in `modules/proj/state-machine.yaml`: states + allowed transitions + required roles.
nfrs.target: 100% of issue state transitions match the FSM; 0 illegal transitions in production

nfrs.title: PROJ blocker detector latency — blocker MUST surface within 4h of cause
nfrs.description: The blocker detector (`FR-PROJ-011`) MUST surface blockers (issues stalled > 3d, missing assignee, missing estimate, etc.) within 4h of the triggering condition becoming true.
nfrs.target: p95 < 4h from blocker condition to dashboard surfacing

nfrs.title: RES matrix-trigger latency — over/under flags MUST surface within 1h of capacity change
nfrs.description: The capacity-demand matrix (`FR-RES-001`) MUST refresh on allocation, hiring, or scope mutations within 1h.
nfrs.target: p95 < 1h from allocation change to over/under flag visibility

nfrs.title: RES allocation-change history — every allocation mutation MUST emit an audit row
nfrs.description: Every change to a member's allocation (create, update, deactivate) MUST emit a memory audit row carrying `{member_id, project_id, before_pct, after_pct, actor_id, reason?, changed_at}`.
nfrs.target: 100% of allocation mutations produce an audit row with before/after delta

nfrs.title: RES OT consent capture — VN overtime MUST require + persist worker consent
nfrs.description: VN-resident workers' overtime MUST NOT be allocated without explicit, recorded consent of the worker, per the VN labour code OT cap (`FR-RES-005`).
nfrs.target: 100% of VN overtime hours carry recorded worker consent + supervisor signoff

nfrs.title: RES hiring-memo signoff — CUO-generated memo MUST have hiring-manager + CHRO co-sign
nfrs.description: The hiring-memo CUO workflow (`FR-RES-004`) MUST produce a memo signed by both the hiring manager + CHRO before the role is published to recruiting channels.
nfrs.target: 100% of hiring memos carry both signatures before role goes to recruiting

nfrs.title: RES weekly digest freshness — Monday digest MUST include data through Sunday EOD
nfrs.description: The Monday capacity digest MUST include allocation data current through the prior Sunday 23:59 (tenant local time).
nfrs.target: 100% of Monday digests include data current through prior Sunday 23:59 local tenant time

nfrs.title: RES capacity-flag accuracy — flag MUST match human-confirmed over/under state ≥ 95% of time
nfrs.description: The capacity flags (over-alloc, under-alloc) MUST match COO's human assessment of the same member ≥ 95% of the time over a 4-week rolling sample.
nfrs.target: Flag agreement with COO confirmation ≥ 95% over a 4-week sample
nfrs.measurement: Weekly: COO samples 10 flags + 10 unflagged; computes agreement rate.

nfrs.title: RES member-departure handling — terminated members MUST be removed from active allocations within 24h
nfrs.description: When a member's HR record transitions to `terminated` with effective date D, all active allocations MUST be ended by D + 24h.
nfrs.target: 100% of terminated members removed from active allocations within 24h of termination effective date

nfrs.title: REW comp-formula determinism — same inputs MUST produce same payroll output across reruns
nfrs.description: The monthly payroll compute (`FR-REW-005`) MUST produce byte-identical outputs when rerun with the same `{member_set, parameter_version, period, 3p_income_set}` tuple.
nfrs.target: 100% of payroll runs are bit-identical on rerun with same inputs + parameter version
nfrs.measurement: Hash comparison on every recompute; counter `rew_payroll_recompute_hash_mismatch_total` — must be 0 if inputs unchanged.

nfrs.title: REW payroll-cycle accuracy — monthly run MUST close within +/- 1 VND vs hand-verified sample
nfrs.description: Each monthly payroll run MUST be sample-verified: 5 random members' gross/SI/PIT/net amounts hand-computed by the CFO + an external accountant and compared against the system output.
nfrs.target: 100% of monthly runs within ±1 VND of a manual sample of 5 members per period

nfrs.title: REW bonus-pool conservation — pool inflow MUST equal sum of distributions + carry-over
nfrs.description: The bonus-pool ledger (`FR-REW-007`) MUST satisfy: every inflow row sums to the same total as the corresponding outflow rows (distribution) plus the carry-over balance, per quarter.
nfrs.target: 100% conservation: |pool_in - (pool_distributed + carry_over)| < 1 VND per quarter

nfrs.title: REW P3 distribution audit — quarterly distribution MUST have CFO+CHRO co-sign before execution
nfrs.description: Every P3 quarterly distribution MUST be approved by both CFO and CHRO via signed approval rows before execution; single signature is insufficient.
nfrs.target: 100% of P3 distributions carry CFO + CHRO signatures; no auto-execute without both

nfrs.title: REW memory exclusion — comp data MUST NOT land in memory Layer-1 or Layer-2
nfrs.description: Per DEC-036, comp/payroll data (salary, bonus, 3P income, distributions) MUST NOT be written to memory Layer-1 or Layer-2 — REW maintains its own segregated audit log.
nfrs.target: 0 memory rows referencing REW comp tables; CI gate enforces per DEC-036

nfrs.title: REW gross/net reconciliation — gross - deductions MUST equal net for every payslip
nfrs.description: Every payslip row MUST satisfy the identity: `gross_vnd - (pit_vnd + si_employee_vnd + advance_recovery_vnd + other_deductions_vnd) = net_vnd`, within ±1 VND tolerance for rounding.
nfrs.target: 100% of payslips satisfy gross - (PIT + SI + other deductions) = net within ±1 VND

nfrs.title: REW VietQR payroll batch integrity — batch hash MUST match sum of payslip nets
nfrs.description: Each VietQR payroll batch MUST be cryptographically tied to the payslip set it disburses: the batch row carries a SHA-256 hash of the canonical-sorted payslip-net list.
nfrs.target: 100% of VietQR batches verifiable: |sum(batch.amounts) - sum(payslip.nets)| = 0

nfrs.title: REW locale handling — payslip currency + tax tables MUST match member residency
nfrs.description: The REW compute path MUST apply VN PIT + SI tables only to members with `residency = VN`; non-VN residents are flagged out-of-scope and excluded from the standard compute.
nfrs.target: 100% of payslips use the correct VN tax tables; non-VN residents flagged as out-of-scope

nfrs.title: SKILL manifest schema invariants — required-field gate + semver discipline
nfrs.description: Every SKILL bundle published to the OCI registry MUST carry a top-level `SKILL.md` whose YAML frontmatter parses cleanly and contains the closed set of required keys: `name`, `version`, `description`, `inputs`, `outputs`, `capabilities`, `audit`.
nfrs.target: 100% of published manifests pass schema validation; 0 unknown-field tolerance

nfrs.title: SKILL capability broker admit latency — grant decision p95 < 25ms
nfrs.description: The SKILL capability broker MUST answer admit requests (skill requests cap X for tenant Y) at p95 < 25ms, p99 < 75ms, measured at the broker's `/admit` ingress over a 14-day rolling window.
nfrs.target: p95 < 25ms for capability admit (token check + policy lookup + decision)

nfrs.title: SKILL OCI registry availability — 99.95% read SLO for bundle pulls
nfrs.description: The SKILL OCI registry MUST serve manifest reads (`GET /v2/<name>/manifests/<ref>`) and blob reads (`GET /v2/<name>/blobs/<digest>`) at ≥ 99.95% monthly availability measured at the public ingress.
nfrs.target: 99.95% monthly availability for GET /v2/<bundle>/manifests/<ref> + blobs/<digest>
nfrs.measurement: Synthetic prober every 60s from 3 regions hitting `GET /v2/cyberos-skill-fr-author/manifests/latest`.

nfrs.title: SKILL audit-chain coverage — every skill invocation MUST emit ≥ 1 memory row
nfrs.description: Every skill invocation (success, denial, error) MUST result in at least one Layer-1 memory row carrying `{tenant_id, actor_id, skill_name, skill_version, capability_used, started_at, ended_at, outcome, output_digest?}`.
nfrs.target: 100% of skill invocations produce a Layer-1 row within 1s; reconciliation drift < 0.001%

nfrs.title: SKILL VN-bundle compliance — MST/VietQR/HoaDon skills MUST pass external validator
nfrs.description: The three Vietnamese-locale skills (`vn-mst-validate`, `vn-bank-transfer`, `vn-vat-invoice`) MUST pass a weekly end-to-end validation run against their respective external authorities: GDT MST lookup endpoint, NAPAS VietQR specification validator, and GDT hoadondientu sandbox.
nfrs.target: 100% pass rate against GDT MST + NAPAS VietQR + GDT hoadondientu sandboxes weekly
nfrs.measurement: CI metric per skill: `skill_vn_validator_pass_count` and `skill_vn_validator_fail_count` per weekly run.

nfrs.title: SKILL fine-tune loop integrity — author+audit pair MUST stay in lockstep
nfrs.description: Every author skill at `skill/public/<name>/` MUST have a sibling audit skill at `skill/public/<name>-audit/` carrying matching version + same set of declared outputs.
nfrs.target: 0 author skills exist without a sibling audit skill; CI gates publish
nfrs.measurement: CI metric `skill_pair_drift_count` — counts authors without audits; must be 0 to merge.

nfrs.title: SKILL deterministic-output mode — fixed seed produces byte-identical output
nfrs.description: Skills declaring `deterministic: true` in their manifest frontmatter MUST produce byte-identical outputs when invoked twice with the same `{inputs, seed, capabilities}` tuple.
nfrs.target: deterministic-flag skills produce byte-identical outputs over 100 reruns with fixed seed
nfrs.measurement: CI gate per `deterministic: true` skill: rerun 5 times with same seed; assert all outputs hash-match.

nfrs.title: SKILL sandbox isolation — skill execution MUST NOT escape declared capabilities
nfrs.description: A skill MUST NOT access any platform resource (DB, network, filesystem, secrets) outside the capabilities declared in its manifest and granted by the broker.
nfrs.target: 0 cap-bypass incidents in 90 days; 100% of attempted escapes blocked + audited

nfrs.title: SKILL cold-start latency — bundle pull + manifest parse + capability admit < 2s
nfrs.description: First invocation of a skill not already in local cache MUST complete the cold-start steps (`registry pull → manifest parse → schema validate → capability admit → runtime ready`) at p95 < 2s and p99 < 5s.
nfrs.target: p95 < 2s, p99 < 5s for first-invocation of a skill not in cache
nfrs.measurement: Histogram `skill_cold_start_latency_seconds{step=pull|parse|admit|ready}` — surfaces which step is the bottleneck.

nfrs.title: SKILL output contract conformance — produced output MUST match declared schema
nfrs.description: Every skill output MUST match the JSON Schema declared in `outputs:` of its `SKILL.md` frontmatter, validated by the runtime before the output is handed back to the caller.
nfrs.target: 100% of skill outputs validate against their declared output contract

nfrs.title: TEN provisioning saga reliability — every tenant create MUST be transactional
nfrs.description: Tenant provisioning MUST run as a saga with all-or-nothing semantics: every step succeeds and the tenant is committed, OR any step fails and all prior steps roll back.
nfrs.target: 100% of tenant-create sagas reach terminal state (committed | rolled-back); 0 stuck-in-progress

nfrs.title: TEN four-residency pinning — tenant data MUST stay in pinned region; 0 cross-region writes
nfrs.description: Each tenant MUST be pinned to exactly one of the four declared residencies (VN, SG, EU, US) at provision time; the pin is immutable post-provision.
nfrs.target: 100% of tenant writes land in pinned region; cross-region writes audited + blocked

nfrs.title: TEN plan-tier enforcement — over-quota operations MUST be blocked or queued
nfrs.description: Plan tiers (Free, Pro, Business, Enterprise) declare numeric quotas (users, projects, storage GB, API calls/month).
nfrs.target: 100% of plan-quota violations either blocked OR queued with operator-visible state

nfrs.title: TEN 90-day offboarding FSM — termination MUST advance through declared states
nfrs.description: Tenant offboarding MUST advance through the FSM: `requested → grace_30d → final_export → cold_storage_60d → permanent_delete`.
nfrs.target: 100% of offboarded tenants follow the 90-day FSM with declared milestones

nfrs.title: TEN signed bundle determinism — same tenant export MUST produce same hash
nfrs.description: The signed-bundle export (`FR-TEN-105`) MUST be deterministic — re-running with the same `{tenant_id, export_at}` produces byte-identical output (same SHA-256).
nfrs.target: Re-running export with same parameters produces byte-identical bundle hash

nfrs.title: TEN hostile-override approval window — emergency termination requires CEO + CLO co-sign
nfrs.description: Hostile termination (FR-TEN-202) — used when a tenant is committing abuse and the normal 90-day FSM is too slow — MUST require both CEO and CLO-Legal signatures within a 24-hour window.
nfrs.target: 100% of hostile-override terminations carry CEO + CLO signatures within 24h window

nfrs.title: TEN four-axis metering precision — users/storage/AI/seats measured within ±0.1%
nfrs.description: The four metering axes (active users, storage GB-month, AI tokens, seats) MUST report values accurate to within ±0.1% of ground-truth (independent recount from DB).
nfrs.target: Metered amounts accurate within ±0.1% vs ground-truth reconciliation

nfrs.title: TEN audit-event durability — tenant lifecycle events MUST never be lost
nfrs.description: Tenant lifecycle events MUST land in the durable audit store with at-least-once + idempotent semantics.
nfrs.target: 100% of tenant lifecycle events (create/upgrade/offboard/delete) reach the durable audit store

nfrs.title: TEN VND domestic rail uptime — VND-billing path MUST maintain 99.5% monthly availability
nfrs.description: The VND domestic billing rail (NAPAS + bank integration) MUST maintain 99.5% monthly availability for initiation + settlement.
nfrs.target: 99.5% monthly availability for VND-billing initiation + settlement
nfrs.measurement: Synthetic prober (60s cadence) against payment initiation endpoint.
